title: The One With The Male Nanny

jokes.text: Monica enthusiastically screaming about loving her new job after being told she's screaming
jokes.setup: Chandler points out Monica is screaming
jokes.punchline: Monica's response: 'You bet your ass I am!' - doubling down instead of moderating
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica - her obsessive enthusiasm taken to extremes. Good character integration and escalation structure.

jokes.text: Monica declaring Jeffrey 'without a doubt the funniest guy I have ever met' in front of Chandler
jokes.setup: Monica praising her new coworker Jeffrey's humor
jokes.punchline: The implicit threat to Chandler's identity as the funny one
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the episode's B-plot. Monica's obliviousness to how this affects Chandler is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Phoebe needing both hands to hold Mike's hand
jokes.setup: Mike asking if she needs both hands for holding hands
jokes.punchline: Phoebe confirming she does, implying awkward hand-holding technique
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy moment. Phoebe's quirky approach to basic romantic gestures.

jokes.text: If you were bigger you'd hit me
jokes.setup: Mike making Phoebe uncomfortable with relationship talk
jokes.punchline: Mike's self-aware comment about Phoebe's restrained reaction
jokes.explanation: Good economy and self-awareness. Mike acknowledging the tension humorously.

jokes.text: Mike offering gum instead of a key, then finding five dollars
jokes.setup: Mike dramatically presenting something from his pocket
jokes.punchline: It's just gum, then finding money creates another distraction
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection gag with physical comedy elements.

jokes.text: To be honest, I think I'd prefer the five dollars
jokes.setup: Mike finally presenting an apartment key after the gum/money confusion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's deadpan preference for the cash over this romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her unique perspective deflating romantic moments with practical honesty. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: These little things open doors!
jokes.setup: Phoebe marveling at the romantic significance of exchanging keys
jokes.punchline: Her wonder at the basic mechanical function of keys
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - finding childlike wonder in mundane things. High character integration, very quotable.

jokes.text: Do you guys do random drug testing? That's cool. But if you do, I'm gonna need three days' notice.
jokes.setup: Nanny candidate asking about drug testing policy
jokes.punchline: Completely defeating the purpose of 'random' testing by requesting advance notice
jokes.explanation: Good logical absurdity - character doesn't understand the concept of 'random' testing.

jokes.text: She was blond?
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning the first nanny candidate was 'the blond with no bra'
jokes.punchline: Ross only noticed she wasn't wearing a bra, missed her hair color entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - his male gaze completely distracted him from other details.

jokes.text: And she's a little mannish
jokes.setup: Ross reading Sandy's impressive qualifications
jokes.punchline: Sandy appears and is clearly a man, not just 'mannish'
jokes.explanation: Visual reveal gag. Ross's understated reaction to the obvious.

jokes.text: Damn it. All right, I'll call the cab company. We can call them later. Can you just stand there for a moment?
jokes.setup: David realizing he left his luggage in the cab
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's priorities - wanting to look at him is more important than his luggage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's romantic impulsiveness overriding practical concerns.

jokes.text: There's an old Russian expression... Roughly translated, that means: 'This thing that I'm looking at: Wow.'
jokes.setup: David starting to quote a presumably profound Russian expression
jokes.punchline: The 'ancient wisdom' is just a basic compliment in awkward academic language
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - David's academic pretensions making simple compliments overly complex. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: You should see me when... Actually, no, I look pretty good.
jokes.setup: Phoebe starting to be self-deprecating about her appearance
jokes.punchline: Catching herself and deciding she looks good as-is
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness moment for Phoebe - subverting expected self-deprecation with confidence.

jokes.text: Well, that would've been your window.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining she said 'no' when David asked if she was seeing someone
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact observation about the missed opportunity for honesty
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical, slightly sarcastic response to Phoebe's romantic confusion.

jokes.text: Really? Chandler?
jokes.setup: Phoebe calling David 'irresistible'
jokes.punchline: Monica's incredulous comparison to her own choice in Chandler
jokes.explanation: Monica's defensive reaction revealing her own relationship insecurities through humor.

jokes.text: Yeah. You know, and given my lifelong search for irony... you can imagine how happy I am!
jokes.setup: Monica commenting on the ironic timing of David's return
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic response about her 'lifelong search for irony'
jokes.explanation: Great sarcastic delivery from Phoebe - using intellectual language ironically to express frustration.

jokes.text: I knew I should've had this conversation with Joey.
jokes.setup: Monica giving Phoebe moral advice about telling David the truth
jokes.punchline: Phoebe wishing she'd asked Joey, who would give terrible moral advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character callback - we know Joey would tell her to lie or make it worse somehow.

jokes.text: What do you know? You're a door. You just like knock-knock jokes. Save it for inside.
jokes.setup: Chandler talking to himself about being funny
jokes.punchline: Insulting his own door and dismissing its comedy preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Chandler's insecurity leading him to have a comedic argument with an inanimate object. Perfect character integration and quotability.

jokes.text: Hey, yeah, I'm from the waistband of Wyoming. But when I was 7, we headed over to the crotch.
jokes.setup: Chandler's bit about Oklahoma having a panhandle and other states having body parts
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly inappropriate body part geography
jokes.explanation: Good escalating wordplay bit typical of Chandler's observational comedy style.

jokes.text: You know, your new poodle could be your noodle. And fried chicken could be fricken. Waiter? Waiter, excuse me, I'll have the fricken!
jokes.setup: Chandler's bit about combining words like 'y'all'
jokes.punchline: The restaurant scene with 'fricken' as the climax
jokes.explanation: Chandler's extended wordplay bit with good escalation to the restaurant punchline.

jokes.text: Well, he did this bit... it was Liza Minnelli locked in our freezer eating a raw chicken.
jokes.setup: Monica describing Jeffrey's comedy bit
jokes.punchline: The completely random and bizarre image of the Liza Minnelli bit
jokes.explanation: Bizarre and memorable image, though it's more random than crafted. High originality but low structure.

jokes.text: No, but it sounds fricken funny.
jokes.setup: Monica describing Jeffrey's bit and asking if Chandler was there
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically using his own 'fricken' joke while clearly being hurt
jokes.explanation: Good callback with emotional subtext - Chandler using his own material while processing being replaced.

jokes.text: Are you gay?
jokes.setup: Sandy expressing sensitive feelings about childcare
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt, inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Peak Ross awkwardness - his masculine insecurity making him ask inappropriate questions.

jokes.text: Skadandy? That was his name for me. I'll see you every day right in...
jokes.setup: Sandy telling a heartwarming story about a child he cared for
jokes.punchline: Sandy breaking down emotionally, making Ross uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from the contrast between Sandy's emotional openness and Ross's discomfort.

jokes.text: Yeah, kids say all kinds of crap.
jokes.setup: Sandy's emotional breakdown about meaningful connection with a child
jokes.punchline: Ross's dismissive, emotionally tone-deaf response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - his emotional immaturity reducing a touching moment to crude dismissal.

jokes.text: What kind of a job is that for a man? A nanny? I mean, it's like if a woman wanted to be... Yes? King?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to find an equivalent inappropriate job for women
jokes.punchline: Ross's weak example of 'King' after being challenged
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Ross's flawed logic crumbling under scrutiny. His gender assumptions exposed.

jokes.text: I used some of my homemade lotion on Emma. It's a mixture of calendula and honey cream... Plus, it keeps the hands young.
jokes.setup: Sandy doing excellent childcare
jokes.punchline: The traditionally feminine beauty routine knowledge making Ross uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Reinforces the gender role tension - Sandy's expertise in 'feminine' areas.

jokes.text: You gotta be at least bi.
jokes.setup: Sandy showing more traditionally feminine knowledge and sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Ross's continued inability to accept a straight man with these traits
jokes.explanation: Ross's continued masculine insecurity making him unable to process Sandy's existence.

jokes.text: Thank God your livelihood doesn't depend on it.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he's not good at remembering lines
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic comment about Joey being an actor
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm perfectly targeting Joey's career struggles.

jokes.text: Without that, you just got 'lame with women'!
jokes.setup: Joey agreeing that being funny is Chandler's thing
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt assessment of what Chandler has left without humor
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest Joey moment - his direct assessment is both supportive and devastating.

jokes.text: Fire trucks!
jokes.setup: Chandler asking Joey to set up his fire truck joke
jokes.punchline: Joey's random, context-free exclamation
jokes.explanation: Joey's inability to set up jokes properly - his randomness derails Chandler's plan.

jokes.text: But only with me. No, that's not fair. Who cares. Leave him. I don't mean that. Yes, I do. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: David learning Phoebe is happy with Mike
jokes.punchline: David's rapid emotional cycling through different responses
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing David's internal conflict played out loud - his rationality battling his emotions.

jokes.text: Well, just so you know... hearing it wasn't exactly a Vladnik carnival, either.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying telling him was hard
jokes.punchline: David's academic way of expressing emotional pain through made-up cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect David line - his academic vocabulary making even heartbreak sound like a research paper. High character integration.

jokes.text: In Minsk, it's two on each cheek... and one on the lips. Well, if that's what they do in Minsk.
jokes.setup: David suggesting cultural kissing customs
jokes.punchline: Phoebe accepting the obviously made-up custom
jokes.explanation: Good seduction attempt disguised as cultural education - David's cleverness and Phoebe's gullibility.

jokes.text: I can't hear it again! You know, I can't tell it again! And I'm fine never having heard it.
jokes.setup: Sandy telling his romantic proposal story
jokes.punchline: Ross and Rachel's escalating desperation to avoid the emotional story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross and Rachel's shared discomfort with Sandy's emotional openness.

jokes.text: Do you realize that man has cried in our apartment three times? I haven't cried that many times since I moved in!
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on Sandy's emotional displays
jokes.punchline: Ross's admission that Sandy has cried more than he has
jokes.explanation: Great Ross observation revealing both his emotional repression and his discomfort with Sandy's openness.

jokes.text: What kind of a guy makes delicate French cookies? They're not even butch, manly cookies with... You know, with chunks!
jokes.setup: Ross complaining about Sandy making madeleines
jokes.punchline: Ross's definition of appropriately masculine cookies with 'chunks'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross masculine insecurity - even applying gender roles to baked goods. 'Chunks' is a great specific detail.

jokes.text: I gotta tell you, Ross, it's not like you just came in from branding cattle.
jokes.setup: Ross complaining about Sandy being too sensitive
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out Ross isn't exactly a rugged cowboy himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback - Rachel using specific imagery to highlight Ross's hypocrisy about masculinity. Great economy.

jokes.text: But it's nice. And nice is good. And good is not bad. Ergo, we should keep kissing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying they can't keep kissing
jokes.punchline: David's pseudo-logical argument using academic language to justify making out
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character comedy - using formal logic and Latin to rationalize romantic behavior.

jokes.text: Did Rachel tell you we hired a male nanny? I think that's great! Did she tell you he plays the recorder, recites poetry and bakes madeleines?
jokes.setup: Ross asking Joey about the male nanny
jokes.punchline: Ross listing Sandy's activities in increasingly 'feminine' detail
jokes.explanation: Ross building his case against Sandy by listing stereotypically non-masculine activities.

jokes.text: Really? Guys do that? That's weird. That's like a woman wanting to be... What? What's the end of that sentence?
jokes.setup: Joey starting the same flawed analogy Ross used earlier
jokes.punchline: Monica and Rachel immediately calling him out
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - Joey falling into the same logical trap Ross did, but getting caught faster.

jokes.text: A penis model!
jokes.setup: Joey struggling to find a male-dominated profession equivalent
jokes.punchline: Joey's bizarre and inappropriate answer
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey answer - inappropriate and revealing his limited thinking about gender roles.

jokes.text: Being a good father?
jokes.setup: Monica listing Ross's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Monica's questioning tone suggesting Ross might not be great at fatherhood either
jokes.explanation: Monica's subtle dig at her brother's parenting skills disguised as supportive listing.

jokes.text: You got a man who's a nanny? You got a 'manny'?
jokes.setup: Joey learning about the male nanny
jokes.punchline: Joey's proud creation of the portmanteau 'manny'
jokes.explanation: Joey's simple wordplay joy - he's genuinely pleased with combining 'man' and 'nanny'.

jokes.text: You know, I don't mind a male nanny... but I do draw the line at male wet nurse.
jokes.setup: Joey considering the concept of male nannies
jokes.punchline: Joey's specific boundary at biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - Joey finding the one logical limit to male childcare roles.

jokes.text: So those were pity laughs? Pity laughs?!
jokes.setup: Chandler realizing Monica was forcing laughter
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified repetition and escalating outrage
jokes.explanation: Chandler's ego being crushed - his worst fear about his comedy confirmed.

jokes.text: There once was a man named Chandler whose wife made him die inside.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he does limericks
jokes.punchline: His 'limerick' that's really just expressing his current emotional pain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Chandler using the limerick format to express genuine hurt while proving he can do limericks. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Hey! I made up that joke and told it to you. Not knowing when to shut up. Yep, that's my thing.
jokes.setup: Monica crediting Joey with one of Chandler's jokes
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately giving credit back and identifying his own flaw
jokes.explanation: Great meta-moment with Joey showing unexpected self-awareness about his tendency to reveal too much.

jokes.text: We just happen to wear the same shade.
jokes.setup: Mike pointing out David has Phoebe's lipstick on his mouth
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's ridiculous explanation about matching lipstick
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurd explanation for obvious evidence - her unique logic trying to defuse the situation.

jokes.text: Don't point your finger at me! Why? What are you gonna do about it?
jokes.setup: David and Mike's confrontation escalating
jokes.punchline: The absurdly dramatic reaction to finger pointing
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of mild conflict - both men being overly dramatic about minor gestures.

jokes.text: Well, if you do, come in the spring. It's just lovely there.
jokes.setup: Mike threatening to come to Minsk
jokes.punchline: David's polite tourism advice in the middle of a confrontation
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his academic politeness interrupting the masculine posturing with genuine travel advice.

jokes.text: Really? Sounded like 'Three Blind Mice'. 'Three Blind Mice' goes like this.
jokes.setup: Joey proudly playing 'Hot Cross Buns' on recorder
jokes.punchline: Ross's correction and Joey's confident demonstration of the wrong song
jokes.explanation: Musical comedy moment showing Joey's confidence despite being wrong, and Ross's need to correct.

jokes.text: Who wants to be Mr. Wigglemunch and who's gonna be the Grumpus?
jokes.setup: Sandy introducing puppet characters
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous names 'Mr. Wigglemunch' and 'the Grumpus'
jokes.explanation: Silly puppet names that sound appropriately childish and ridiculous to adult ears.

jokes.text: I want to be Mr. Wigglemunch!
jokes.setup: Sandy asking who wants to play with puppets
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic participation while Ross watches in horror
jokes.explanation: Joey's childlike enthusiasm contrasting with Ross's masculine discomfort - good character contrast.

jokes.text: Well, I guess we know who's gonna be the Grumpus!
jokes.setup: Ross clearly being uncomfortable and negative about the puppet activity
jokes.punchline: Sandy's cheerful assignment of Ross to the grumpy puppet role
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - Sandy accurately assigning puppet roles based on personality.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I'm the only one who isn't in love with Gary Poppins out there.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his dislike of Sandy
jokes.punchline: Ross's reference combining 'Gary' with 'Mary Poppins'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau showing Ross's resentment - combining the male nanny with the famous fictional nanny.

jokes.text: So if your maître d' friend has any funny Oklahoma jokes... tell him to e-mail me at www-dot-ha-ha-not-so-much-dot-com!
jokes.setup: Chandler leaving for Tulsa, still bitter about Jeffrey
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate fake email address expressing his resentment
jokes.explanation: Chandler's passive-aggressive humor disguised as helpfulness - typical of his sarcastic style.

jokes.text: Here goes. I can't watch. It's like firing Elmo.
jokes.setup: Rachel about to fire Sandy
jokes.punchline: Rachel comparing Sandy to the beloved children's character
jokes.explanation: Good comparison highlighting how innocent and likeable Sandy is - making the firing feel cruel.

jokes.text: Oh, damn you, Geller!
jokes.setup: Sandy saying he chose them because he liked them best
jokes.punchline: Rachel's frustrated blame of Ross for making this harder
jokes.explanation: Rachel's frustration at Ross making the firing emotionally difficult - good relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: I play squash!
jokes.setup: Ross admitting he wasn't athletic as a kid
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive claim about current athletic ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross defensive moment - squash being a stereotypically non-rugged sport undermines his point.

jokes.text: Playing and learning.
jokes.setup: Ross describing playing with dinosaurs as a child
jokes.punchline: Ross's need to justify childhood play as educational
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - even as a child he needed to intellectualize everything. Shows his defensive academic nature.

jokes.text: And when it's summer and it's hot, why can't you wear a tank top?!
jokes.setup: Ross having an emotional breakthrough about masculine expectations
jokes.punchline: Ross's specific complaint about tank top restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's emotional breakthrough - the specific tank top detail makes it both funny and poignant.

jokes.text: It's all right. Crying is good. It lets the boo-hoos out.
jokes.setup: Ross crying after his emotional breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Sandy's childlike terminology for crying
jokes.explanation: Sandy's gentle, childcare-appropriate language for emotional moments - 'boo-hoos' is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: And what's the one kind of boat that can never ever sink? What kind? A friend ship!
jokes.setup: Sandy telling a joke to baby Emma
jokes.punchline: The classic children's pun about friendship
jokes.explanation: Standard children's joke - not original but appropriate for Sandy's character and the childcare context.

jokes.text: Wow! You blow my mind! I gotta go. How much do I owe you? Twenty bucks. It's like the cheapest college ever!
jokes.setup: Joey being amazed by Sandy's simple children's joke
jokes.punchline: Joey treating childcare as educational service and comparing it to college tuition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his simple mind genuinely amazed by basic wordplay, then treating Sandy like a tutor. Great character integration.
